title: Imperial Sanctum
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Youmay|the lower throne room in the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]|the primary [[Imperial Throne Room|throne room]] or the [[Imperial Palace audience chamber/Legends|audience chamber]] within the same building, the temporary [[Imperial throne room (Emperor Palpatine Surgical Reconstruction Center)|chamber]] within the [[Emperor Palpatine Surgical Reconstruction Center|EmPal SuRecon facility]], the [[Emperor's throne room (Death Star)/Legends|throne room]] aboard the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]], or the [[Emperor's Throne Room/Legends|set of rooms]] on the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]]; all of which served as the seat of power under [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]}}

The '''Imperial Sanctum''' of the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] served as the seat of the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] following the death of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|battle]] over [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. A lower throne room beneath the [[Imperial Throne Room|chamber]] atop the Palace, the Sanctum served as the private residence of the Emperor during his reign.

==History==
[[File:IsardandPestage.jpg|thumb|left|100px|Emperor [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] and [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] discuss the fate of the Empire.]]

The Imperial Sanctum, the private residence and working offices of the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], was constructed in the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] during the refurbishment of [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]]. Consisting of several chambers including the Emperor's private office and bed chamber, a throne room, and the chambers of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], the suite of chambers were used primarily by Palpatine, Vader, and their functionaries and aide-de-camps.<ref name="JT">''[[Coruscant Nights I: Jedi Twilight]]''</ref> The Emperor and Vader would often invite guests, such as [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] of [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], to discuss things of import within these rooms.<ref name="SOE" />

Following the Emperor and Vader's deaths at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|battle]] over [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] the throne room in the Sanctum was placed in a state of formal mourning; a black shroud was draped over the throne during this turbulent time. When [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] declared himself Emperor, he took up residence in the Sanctum and sat on the lower throne, not daring to ascend to the higher throne room in fear of outright dissension by other Imperials. During this time [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] escaped her prison in the Palace and entered the Sanctum where she stole the black shroud from the throne room and used it to cloak herself as she escaped the Palace.<ref name="Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand">''[[Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand]]''</ref>

After Pestage's overthrow, the Tribunal governed from the chamber where the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]] had once sat. Subsequently, [[Director of Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] used her powers as head of the security apparatus to remove all rivals and seize control of the entire administration, making herself de facto Empress, but she eschewed the formal trappings of power, and her existing office took over the functions of the throne room as a control center and high-level audience chamber.

==Layout==
A far more sparse environment than the summit throne room, the command chamber was vast and cavernous. The throne itself jettied out on a long, blade-like balcony in a vast empty space; the only entrance lay at the far end of the balcony guarded by a pair of the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guards]].

Other chambers in the Sanctum included the Emperor's working office which was unadorned save for a desk and a [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] and several pieces of prized artwork. Similarly, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] office was kept bare but for a series of [[Datascreen/Legends|datascreens]] that kept him up to date on galactic happenings from across the Empire.
